(9) Portents of the Wind.
^ftgqci qft \
If a carriage moves without a driver (or horses), or does not
move even when driven (or dragged) by him (or by them), or if
the wheels sink into the earth or break down, there is imminent
danger to the kingdom.
[The woid qR may also be interpreted as an animal used for
riding, such as the horse and elephant. Why are the horses brought

Povienlous Phenomena XLVI
387
under this section dealing with the 'portents of the wind' ? Accord-
ing to Vedic literature the horses are presided over by the Wind-
God. It is also important to note that the'speed of Wind' is an
exemplar for that of running animals. Likewise, sounds are also
carried by the wind.]
When sounds of singing and musical instruments are heard
in the sky; or when moving objects (like carriages) become station-
ary, and stationary objects (like trees) move, it augurs death or
diseases.
